messages.content: ⚠ No Squad team found. Run /init to create your team first.
🏗️ No team yet — casting one based on your project...

Team proposed:

👤  Rusty      — Project Manager / Planner scope, sequencing, delivery
👤  Linus      — Brainstormer    wild ideas, hooks
⚛️  Livingston — Frontend Dev    Vue, UX, polish
🔧  Danny      — Backend Dev     APIs, services, data
🧪  Basher     — Tester          QA, automation, edge-cases
📋  Scribe     — (silent)        Memory, decisions, session logs
🔄  Ralph      — (monitor)       Work queue, backlog, keep-alive

Universe: Ocean's Eleven
Look good? Type **y** to confirm or **n** to cancel.
📌 Routing your message to the team now...

Team initialized. I’ll route scope/sequencing/delivery to **Danny**, ideas/hooks to **Rusty**, frontend/UX to **Linus**, backend/APIs/data to **Basher**, and QA/automation/edge-cases to **Livingston**, with the squad framed in **Ocean’s Eleven**.

❌ Something went wrong processing your message.
   Try:
   • Try your message again
   • Run 'squad doctor' for diagnostics
   • Check your internet connection

I’m picking up the current workspace state and checking what’s already in flight before I verify the app end to end.I found a fresh, mostly untracked full-stack app, so I’m checking the handoff notes and startup requirements before running it.The scaffold is there and the last squad session died during routing, so I’m validating the actual code paths directly now.I’ve confirmed it’s just the scaffold, so I’m doing the practical verification now: build, then boot both services and hit them.There’s one real blocker: the backend build is failing on a TypeScript 6 config deprecation, so I’m fixing that before I re-run the full verification.

I’ve isolated the blocker to the backend TypeScript config. I’m tracking that fix and the runtime verification as separate steps so I can finish the handoff cleanly.The config fix is in. I’m rerunning the workspace build now, then I’ll boot both apps and hit them directly.Builds are clean now. I’m checking for existing listeners first so I don’t trample anything in this shared environment before I start the app.Both processes are launching in the background. I’m giving them a moment and then probing the backend health check and the frontend HTTP endpoint.The app responded on both ports, so I’m closing the temporary servers and marking verification complete.DIRECT: **It runs.** I fixed the backend TypeScript 6 blocker by adding `"ignoreDeprecations": "6.0"` to `backend/tsconfig.json`, then rebuilt and started both apps successfully.

The backend responded at `http://127.0.0.1:4000/health` with `{"status":"ok"}`, and the frontend served `http://127.0.0.1:4173/` with `HTTP/1.1 200 OK`. Temporary verification servers have been stopped.DIRECT: Noted — those were the temporary verification processes, and they’re already shut down.

**Yes — it runs successfully when started.** I verified the backend at `http://127.0.0.1:4000/health` returned `{"status":"ok"}` and the frontend at `http://127.0.0.1:4173/` returned `200 OK`.

The temporary verification processes were stopped afterward, so it is **not currently left running** in the background.
